recovery-http.description: D-Link recovery mode (hold reset pin under device while powering on; works on both WAN and LAN ports)
recovery-http.reset_instructions: Connect ethernet to any port (WAN or LAN). Use a pin to press and hold the reset button UNDER the device. Plug in power while holding reset. Hold for ~10-12 seconds until LED blinks red, then release.

dlink-hnap.description: D-Link stock firmware HNAP SOAP API firmware upload (no reset needed, works when router is running stock firmware)
dlink-hnap.notes: HNAP challenge-response auth with HMAC-MD5 + custom AES (HNAP_CONTENT). Login with default password, upload factory image via multipart POST. WARNING: GetFirmwareValidation rejects OpenWrt images on stock v1.02 (IsValid=false). Use recovery-http for reliable flashing.
dlink-hnap.caveat: Firmware upload API returns OK but stock firmware validation blocks non-D-Link images. The GPL RSA signing key is a test key, not the production key. Recovery-http (U-Boot) bypasses all validation.

reboot.recovery_mac_last_packet: TCP FIN after HTTP response body

sticker_credentials.description: Factory WiFi SSID/password and MAC addresses stored in NAND config2 partition

known_keys.factory_mac: LAN MAC address printed on device sticker

sticker_credentials.ssid_format: dlink-XXXX where XXXX is last 4 hex chars of MAC or random

recovery-http.notes: Flashed from macOS via recovery mode (hold reset pin, power on). ~1m47s total.

dlink-hnap.notes: Auth + upload works (returns OK). GetFirmwareValidation returns IsValid=false for OpenWrt images — stock firmware validation blocks non-D-Link firmware. Recovery-http is the proven method.

sysupgrade.notes: Standard OpenWrt sysupgrade - not yet tested on this model

wifi_sta_ap.notes: Post-flash WiFi config verified: radio0=2g, radio1=5g. STA only, AP only, STA+AP all verified on real hardware.
